label: The Connector
gift: Relational gravity — the pull that brings people into each other’s orbit.

steps: Notice what is actually here, sensing what is here.

cards.name: Map the Network
cards.essence: See the whole web of people who could help — who can act, who can connect you onward, who can amplify.
steps: List everyone in reach who cares, in three tiers: help directly, connect you onward, amplify.
Spot the one connector who could open ten more doors — and notice who you flinch to ask.
Name the highest-leverage ask first.
cards.primaryQuestion: Whose support am I overlooking because asking them feels too exposing?
cards.campaignQuestion: Who can help directly, who can connect, who can amplify — and where's the leverage?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing latent reach before grinding out one-by-one asks.
forbiddenMoves: Ignoring people who'd want to help
failureModes: Tunnel vision on the obvious few
Missing the person who'd rally others
cards.remediation: If stuck, ask one person 'who else do you know who'd want in on this?'

tell.working: The map feels bigger and more alive.
tell.performed: You make a list but ask no one on it.

contraindications: When you're using mapping to avoid the actual ask
cards.example: Realize an old teammate runs a group chat of 40 — one warm intro could matter more than ten DMs.

cards.name: The Warm Ask
cards.essence: Open a real ask to someone who'd want to help — without turning the relationship into a ledger.
steps: List the people who'd be glad to be asked.
For each, name the specific thing you'd ask for and a true reason you feel in your body.
Ask one today, in your own voice, with an explicit no-strings out.
cards.primaryQuestion: Whose help am I afraid to receive, and what story makes the ask feel like a debt?
cards.campaignQuestion: Who would genuinely want a chance to help with this — and how do I invite them cleanly?
cards.optimizesFor: Support that flows through warmth, not pressure.
failureModes: Only asking the people who least mind
cards.remediation: If it curdles, say 'no strings — really,' and mean it.

tell.working: You feel cleaner after asking, win or lose.
tell.performed: You start tracking who now owes you.

contraindications: When there's no real warmth in the relationship yet
cards.example: Message one person who'd want in: name the specific thing and a clear amount or task, with a genuine 'no worries if not.'

Shadow check: the Overextended Hub — everything routing through you.

cards.name: Clear the Static
cards.essence: Clear the relational static with someone before you ask, so the ask lands clean.
steps: Notice the unspoken charge with this person (old debts, awkwardness).
Name it lightly to yourself, then feel where it sits in your body.
Ask only once the static has dropped a notch.
cards.primaryQuestion: What old relational charge am I about to route into this ask?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's unspoken between us that would make the ask land wrong if I ignore it?
cards.optimizesFor: An ask received as warmth, not as the next move in an old pattern.
failureModes: The ask reopens an old wound
cards.remediation: If static spikes mid-ask, pause and name it: 'I know it's been a while — no pressure here.'

tell.performed: You rehearse the ask to manage their reaction.

contraindications: When the relationship needs repair before any ask
cards.example: Before reaching out to someone you've drifted from, feel the old charge, then lead with reconnection, not the ask.

cards.name: The Direct Ask
cards.essence: Make the one concrete ask that moves the work today — specific person, specific request, by when.
steps: Pick the person and the specific request.
Make the ask plainly, with a deadline.
Make it easy to say yes — and easy to say no.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I feeling in my gut right before I make the ask — and am I about to soften it?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the single ask that moves things today: to whom, for what, by when?
cards.optimizesFor: A real, specific request on the table, not a softened hint.
failureModes: The ask that never quite gets made
cards.remediation: If you softened it, follow up with one line naming the real request.

tell.working: There's a specific request on the table.
tell.performed: You 'raised awareness' but asked no one.

contraindications: When you haven't built any relationship with the person yet
cards.example: Ask directly: 'Could you do [specific thing] by Friday? Totally fine to say no.'

cards.name: Make one tie you will tend — Steward

steps: Take the concrete act, stewarding it over time.
Practice it within: one tie you will tend.

cards.name: Make the introduction that matters — Steward
cards.essence: For others: relational gravity, stewarding it over time.
steps: Take the concrete act, stewarding it over time.
Offer it to others: the introduction that matters.
Shadow check: the Overextended Hub — everything routing through you.

cards.name: Make one tie you will tend — Structure
cards.essence: In yourself: relational gravity, building it into structure.
steps: Take the concrete act, building it into structure.
Practice it within: one tie you will tend.
Shadow check: the Overextended Hub — everything routing through you.

cards.name: Build the Milestone Ladder
cards.essence: Give the goal a structure people can join — milestones, a match, a visible climb.
steps: Break the goal into rungs (25% / 50% / 75% / 100%) with a why for each.
Line up one person to match or double a rung.
Post the ladder so people see the climb and where they fit.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I avoiding building structure because it makes the need feel real?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structure — milestones, a match, a tracker — turns scattered effort into a climb people join?
cards.optimizesFor: Momentum that compounds instead of one-off pushes.
failureModes: Structure that stalls the actual work
cards.remediation: Ship the simplest version today: three rungs and a visible tracker.

tell.working: People start asking how to help.
tell.performed: You tinker with the design to delay asking.

contraindications: When structure is procrastination in disguise
cards.example: Post the first milestone and a matcher: 'Hit the first 25% and a friend matches the next chunk.'

cards.name: Make one tie you will tend — Bridge
cards.essence: In yourself: relational gravity, across difference and relationship.
steps: Take the concrete act, across difference and relationship.
Practice it within: one tie you will tend.
Shadow check: the Overextended Hub — everything routing through you.

cards.name: Invite, Don't Extract
cards.essence: Ask in a way that gives people the gift of helping, with full consent and no strings.
steps: Frame the ask as an invitation into something good.
State the no-strings out loud and feel whether your body agrees.
Thank helpers in a way that completes the loop, not opens a debt.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I about to extract from someone instead of inviting them?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do I invite people to help so they feel chosen, not used?
cards.optimizesFor: Helping that strengthens the relationship instead of taxing it.
failureModes: A yes that quietly costs the friendship
cards.remediation: If you sense pressure, say 'genuinely no strings — I'll love you either way.'

tell.working: Helpers thank YOU for the chance.
tell.performed: You feel a flicker of leverage.

contraindications: When you need it so badly you can't hear a no
cards.example: 'I'm inviting a few people who've had my back to be part of this — zero pressure.'
